eBPF Network Functions Used: XDP packet filter, load balancer, rate limiter, DDoS mitigator
How They Use eBPF: Drops malicious packets at NIC driver level (XDP) before reaching Linux stack; processes terabits/sec of traffic
Use Case 1: Security (Conformance Check): YES — Must verify XDP drop rules correctly encode rulesets; even a small gap = traffic bypass
Use Case 2: Debugging (NF Behavior Under: YES — Built custom xdpcap because standard tools fail with XDP; behavioral querying would replace manual bytecode analysis
Use Case 3: Performance: YES — Operates at line-rate; NF efficiency is critical; any complexity/overhead = performance degradation
Notes / Evidence: Multiple public blog posts on XDP/eBPF. Custom debugging tooling (xdpcap) exists specifically because of NF opacity problem.

eBPF Network Functions Used: L4 load balancer (Katran), security policy enforcer (BpfJailer)
How They Use eBPF: Katran processes ALL ingress traffic across Meta data centers; BpfJailer enforces security isolation; Strobelight profiles CPU
Use Case 1: Security (Conformance Check): YES — BpfJailer conformance is a security-critical requirement; verifying policy NFs protect workloads correctly
Use Case 2: Debugging (NF Behavior Under: YES — At data center scale, understanding NF behavior across billions of daily flows requires systematic tooling
Use Case 3: Performance: YES — Katran saves significant CPU vs. traditional LB; NF performance analysis is part of their cost optimization
Notes / Evidence: Open-source Katran available on GitHub. Katran paper published at Facebook Engineering. BpfJailer used in production.

Use Case 1: Security (Conformance Check): YES — Tetragon TracingPolicy conformance: verifying that security enforcement NFs correctly block prohibited actions
Use Case 2: Debugging (NF Behavior Under: YES — Hubble shows flows but not NF behavioral logic; Yaksha Prashna adds querying of the enforcement logic itself
Use Case 3: Performance: YES — Cilium's design is optimized for performance; verifying NF behavior helps optimize policy rule chains
Notes / Evidence: Used as default CNI on AWS EKS. Tetragon open-sourced. Part of CNCF. Acquired by Cisco 2023.
